domain_focus: DocRED document-level relation extraction from Wikipedia-style text. Treat the entire document as one semantic unit. Extract only useful DocRED endpoint mentions and relation-bearing spans; avoid peripheral topical phrases that cannot participate in a target relation. Preserve proper names, dates, countries, cities, organizations, persons, works, aliases, pronouns, demonyms and cross-sentence evidence. TARGET RELATION PLAYBOOK: P17 country (subject non-human entity/place -> sovereign country); P27 country of citizenship (person -> country of citizenship); P30 continent (place/country -> continent); P31 instance of (instance -> class/type); P69 educated at (person -> educational institution attended); P108 employer (person -> employer); P127 owned by (owned entity/organization -> owner or owning group); P131 located in the administrative territorial entity (place/entity -> containing administrative territorial entity); P150 contains the administrative territorial entity (administrative entity -> directly contained subdivision); P159 headquarters location (organization -> headquarters/base city or location); P175 performer (creative work -> performer); P264 record label (musical work/artist -> record label); P355 has subsidiary (parent organization -> subsidiary); P361 part of (part/item -> larger whole or series; use only when a more specific corporate property is not supported); P463 member of (person/organization -> organization or consortium of which it is a member); P527 has part(s) (whole -> component/part); P569 date of birth (person -> birth date); P570 date of death (person -> death date); P571 inception (organization/work/event -> inception or establishment date); P577 publication date (creative work -> publication/release date); P749 parent organization (subsidiary/branch -> parent organization) IMPORTANT RELATION-INSTANCE RULE: Layer 4 produces at most one assertion per relation mention, so extract separate relation-bearing expressions for each independently supported source-target pair.
abstraction_level: shallow, entity-centric, exact-surface, ontology-canonical relation extraction
priority_relations: P131 : located in the administrative territorial entity
P150 : contains the administrative territorial entity
population_policy: Named entities are instances and must keep their source surface labels; never rename them to generic labels such as entity, organization, location, date or country. Relation-bearing expressions must be linked to one canonical DocRED property whenever ontology evidence supports it. REQUIRED HINT CONTRACT FOR RELATIONS: ontology_hints must contain "controlled_relation:Pxxx : canonical label" and "promote_to_ontology:true"; include domain/range hints when available and preserve the lexical phrase as an alias. Corporate disambiguation: membership=P463; explicit owner/owned-by=P127; subsidiary-to-parent=P749; parent-to-subsidiary=P355; generic part-whole=P361 only when no more specific corporate property is supported. For organization + stated base/headquarters city prefer P159. Use P17 for non-human entity/place to country and P27 only for people. Controlled high-confidence document-level inference is allowed for aliases/coreference, demonyms, “the country”, and common city-country containment; mark such links as inferred in the justification. Do not add a second extractor, source anchoring, closure rules, or gold-derived facts. Because Layer 4 is one-assertion-per-relation-mention, do not collapse several relation instances into one generic predicate candidate; preserve a separate evidence-bearing relation expression for each pair, including separate temporal and locative spans from the same sentence.
event_modeling_preference: DocRED is entity-centric. Dates and locations remain endpoint entities. Extract a relation span with enough wording to retain all arguments, e.g. “was relaunched ... in” rather than separating the predicate from its date/location evidence. It is acceptable to extract the same source token as both an endpoint mention and relation evidence when a demonym or apposition supports a relation.

typing_examples.explanation: A date is a relation endpoint.

typing_examples.text: is part of the media group
typing_examples.explanation: Corporate relation phrase requiring ontology disambiguation.
The demonym can be retained as evidence for a country relation while the named broadcaster remains an entity.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Television is based in Harbor City.
relation_examples.explanation: Organization-to-base-city direction. Keep the exact entities and canonicalize the predicate to P159.

relation_examples.text: Harbor City is in Republic Z.
relation_examples.explanation: Use P17 for a non-human place/entity to its sovereign country.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Television is part of Northstar Media Group.
relation_examples.explanation: In a corporate/group context, test ownership or parent-organization semantics before generic P361.

relation_examples.text: Northstar Research Europe is a subsidiary of Northstar Research.
Northstar Research has subsidiary Northstar Research Europe.
The broadcaster is a member of the Digital Networks Alliance.
relation_examples.explanation: Membership is not ownership, parent organization, or generic part-of.

relation_examples.text: The institute was established in June 2010.
Alex Morgan studied at Central College.
The single was performed by Artist Q.
The single was released by Aurora Records.

promotion_examples.explanation: Canonical DocRED relation schemas are promoted.
Named instances are not promoted to ontology classes.

negative_examples.explanation: Do not replace a named entity with a generic class label.

negative_examples.text: organization is part of a corporate group -> always P361
city + country co-occur with no evidence or common-knowledge support
negative_examples.explanation: Do not invent uncertain geographic links.

negative_examples.text: extract every topical phrase in the article
negative_examples.explanation: Keep Layer 1 focused on target endpoints and relation spans to reduce cost and noise.

negative_examples.text: omit a country because it appears in a later sentence
negative_examples.explanation: The whole document is the semantic unit.

negative_examples.text: one generic country relation mention for several different source entities
negative_examples.explanation: Preserve separate evidence expressions so native Layer 4 can evaluate each endpoint pair.
